payload.description: Transform Woodhaven and Cross Bay Boulevards into a complete street where buses operate quickly and reliably, bus customers safely access stations, pedestrians are comfortable, and drivers travel at reasonable and safe speeds. Community Advisory Committee Meeting #3.

warnings: project.lead_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Lead agency is relation context. Suggested relation: implemented_by.
project.partner_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Partner agency is relation context. Suggested relation: has_partner.

payload.description: Limited-stop bus route on Woodhaven / Cross Bay corridor. Q52 Limited operates between Elmhurst & Arverne. Proposed SBS route based on existing Q52 LTD route.
Limited-stop bus route on Woodhaven / Cross Bay corridor. Proposed SBS route based on existing Q52/53 LTD bus route.

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.description: Based on the existing Q52/53 LTD bus route, 14 miles long from Woodside to the Rockaways. Within a 15-minute walk of the corridor: 400,000 residents, 43% of households do not own a car, 60% of residents commute by transit.

Series of 2014 community outreach meetings including CAC #1 (Feb 12), Queens Metropolitan HS Meeting (Mar 11), Public Workshop #1 (Apr 23), CB10 Presentation (Jun 5), Public Workshop #2 (Jun 25), Rockaways Public Workshop (Sep 18), CAC #2 (Oct 22), Public Workshop #3 (Nov 5)

April 2015: Present draft corridor design plans at public design workshops. Summer 2015: Refine design plans based on community feedback. Fall 2015: Transfer project to NYC Department of Design and Construction for Final Design and engineering.
Public design workshops: April 16 - Woodhaven Blvd from Union Tpke to Rockaway Blvd; April 23 - Woodhaven Blvd from Queens Blvd to Union Tpke; April 29 - Cross Bay Blvd; April 30 - The Rockaways.
Existing Conditions & Analysis (2014), 3 Design Concepts & Screening (2014), Preferred Corridor Design (2015), Final Design & Engineering (2016-2017)

tool_args.label: Woodhaven Blvd - Main Road Bus Lanes

payload.description: Main road bus lanes along Woodhaven Boulevard. All buses use median stations.

tool_args.label: Cross Bay Blvd - Offset Bus Lanes

payload.description: Offset bus lanes on Cross Bay Boulevard north of 165 Av. SBS buses stop at bus bulbs. Local buses stop at the curb.
Designated bus-only station areas at Queens Blvd and Hoffman Dr. Improved bus stops / transfers.
No bus lanes. Targeted transit priority treatments. Improved curbside bus stops.

tool_args.label: Over 6 Miles of Continuous Bus Lanes

payload.description: Over 6 miles of continuous bus lanes with opportunity to explore unique treatments along Woodhaven Boulevard including physical separation (hard barriers, soft barriers like rumble strips) and bus lane materials.

tool_args.label: Cross Bay Blvd Bus Bulb Layout

payload.description: Typical bus bulb layout for Cross Bay Boulevard. SBS buses stop at the bus bulb. Local / Express buses stop at the curb. Maneuvering space between SBS and Local/Express stops.

payload.description: 400,000 residents within a 15-minute walk of the Woodhaven / Cross Bay corridor

tool_args.label: 43% of Households Do Not Own a Car

payload.raw_value_text: 43% of households do not own a car
payload.description: 43% of households within a 15-minute walk of the corridor do not own a car
60% of residents within a 15-minute walk of the corridor commute by transit

payload.claim_text: Community feedback identified: 1. Bus service is unreliable and slow during rush hour 2. Transit improvements are needed to better serve customers, especially in the Rockaways 3. Pedestrian crossings are long and dangerous 4. Congestion leads to long and difficult trips for buses and drivers 5. Changing road widths and configurations make the corridor difficult to navigate
payload.subject: Woodhaven / Cross Bay corridor
payload.description: Community feedback issues collected during outreach process

payload.claim_text: Concept 1 (Offset Bus Lanes): Good design for Cross Bay Boulevard, median refuge improves pedestrian safety. Conflicts: too much free access for drivers to block bus lanes for right turns, deliveries, and finding parking; conflicts with driveways; less desirable for bus operations.
payload.subject: Concept 1 - Offset Bus Lanes
payload.description: Screening community input for Concept 1 from CAC Meeting #2 (Oct 22, 2014) and Public Workshop (Nov 5, 2014)

payload.claim_text: Concept 2 (Main Road Bus Lanes): Balances transit and pedestrian access, calms service roads and removes bike/bus conflict, creates comfortable and safe bus stations, keeps Woodhaven consistent with main and service roads. Concerns about left turn bans and congestion especially during rush hours.
payload.subject: Concept 2 - Main Road Bus Lanes
payload.description: Screening community input for Concept 2 from CAC Meeting #2 (Oct 22, 2014) and Public Workshop (Nov 5, 2014)

payload.claim_text: Concept 3 (Median Bus Lanes): 'Provides the most benefits to bus riders', might have ability to get people out of cars and on the bus, clearly defines where vehicles should travel on road. Concerns about pedestrian safety at center bus lanes and median stations, too many passing lanes required for local bus stops at non-SBS spots, potential loss of parking.
payload.subject: Concept 3 - Median Bus Lanes
payload.description: Screening community input for Concept 3 from CAC Meeting #2 (Oct 22, 2014) and Public Workshop (Nov 5, 2014)

payload.claim_text: The concepts were presented at CAC Meeting #2 (Oct 22, 2014) and a Public Workshop (Nov 5, 2014)
payload.subject: Design concepts screening
payload.description: Concepts presented to community for feedback

tool_args.label: Proposed SBS Station Changes from Q52/Q53 LTD

payload.claim_text: Changes from the Q52/Q53 LTD stops: SBS stops at 91 Av instead of Atlantic Av (local bus will still stop at Atlantic Av); New stop at 101 Av; New stop at Pitkin Av; Broad Channel and Rockaway stops to be discussed at upcoming workshop.
payload.subject: Proposed SBS stations
payload.description: Changes to stops from existing Q52/Q53 limited service for the proposed SBS route

payload.claim_text: The SBS will use the viaduct over Atlantic Av (local bus will use service roads to access Atlantic Av). Q52 extension is under consideration.
payload.subject: Proposed SBS route
payload.description: Changes to the route alignment from existing Q52/Q53 service

payload.claim_text: MTA Bus is currently studying community requests to extend the Q52 further east in the Rockaway Peninsula. Analysis includes Origin/Destinations, Transfers, Trip Generators, Ridership, and Q52/Q53 - Q22 Transfer Survey performed early March 2015.
payload.subject: Q52 extension
payload.description: Q52 Extension Study to extend service further east in the Rockaway Peninsula

payload.claim_text: Features of Concept 2 (chosen concept): Main road bus lanes improve bus speed and reliability with no conflicts with turning vehicles or parking; high-quality median bus stations for all buses; medians shorten pedestrian crossing distances, provide refuges, and add greenery; calm service roads for parking, deliveries, and local access trips; main roadway for thru vehicle trips; consistent roadway design for the entire corridor improves navigability.
payload.subject: Concept 2 - Main Road Bus Lanes
payload.description: Features of the selected Concept 2 design
